context: Tests whether the agent implements the core dynamic pricing algorithm with the correct demand signal thresholds, inventory pressure logic, competitor undercutting rules, slow-mover markdown, and the two tiers of price guardrails (business floor/ceiling and cost-based margin floor). Also checks that the recommendation and application concerns are separated.

checklist.name: TypeScript interfaces defined
checklist.description: Defines separate named types/interfaces for pricing context input, competitor price, and pricing decision output (three distinct structures)

checklist.name: Prices stored as cents
checklist.description: The interface/type definitions use integer or number fields annotated or documented as cents for currentPrice, costPrice, floorPrice, ceilingPrice (not floating-point dollars)

checklist.name: Demand signal: conversion threshold
checklist.description: High-demand price increase is triggered when conversionRate (salesLast24h divided by max(viewsLast24h, 1)) exceeds 0.08 — uses exactly 0.08 as the threshold

checklist.name: Demand signal: inventory condition
checklist.description: The high-demand +5% increase is only applied when inventoryLevel is above the reorderPoint (not just on conversion rate alone)

checklist.name: Inventory pressure: correct multiplier
checklist.description: Near-stockout condition (inventoryLevel <= reorderPoint * 0.5) triggers a price increase of exactly +8% (multiplied by 1.08, rounded to integer)

checklist.name: Competitor undercut: 2% band
checklist.description: Competitor price reduction is only triggered when current price is more than 2% above the lowest in-stock competitor (threshold: lowestCompetitor * 1.02)

checklist.name: Competitor undercut: 1% below
checklist.description: When the competitor undercut condition fires, the new price is set to lowestCompetitorPrice * 0.99 (1% below, not an exact match)

checklist.name: Slow mover markdown
checklist.description: Products with salesVelocity7d below 0.1 receive a 5% markdown (multiplied by 0.95, rounded to integer)

checklist.name: Business floor and ceiling enforced
checklist.description: Output price is clamped to the provided floorPrice and ceilingPrice values (both upper and lower bounds applied)

checklist.name: 15% margin floor enforced
checklist.description: Output price is always at least costPrice * 1.15 rounded to integer — this floor is applied regardless of floor/ceiling settings

checklist.name: Confidence score varies by competitor data
checklist.description: confidenceScore in the output is higher (e.g. 0.85) when in-stock competitor prices are available vs lower (e.g. 0.6) when no in-stock competitors exist

checklist.name: Recommendation separated from application
checklist.description: The compute/recommend function only returns a decision object — it does NOT directly mutate state, write to a database, or apply the price. A separate function or call site is responsible for applying changes.
